Let's talk
Pitch deck · prepared for Haldiram's · 2026

India already loves Haldiram's. Let's make the feed crave it too.

We're Revamp, a Delhi NCR creative and performance studio that already runs the social, the shoots and the campaigns for fresh food and QSR brands. We'd love to do the same for the name that defined Indian snacking.

See our food brand work How we'd run Haldiram's
Delhi NCR based Live on fresh food & QSR Strategy + studio + ads, one team
What you asked for

Your scope, mapped to exactly what we do.

You said it plainly: you don't want a page management vendor, you want a partner who can think, shoot and convert. Here's how every line of your brief lands with us.

01

Social media for QSR & fresh food pages

Always on calendars, community management and trend jacking, already live and growing for Isthara, Koreatown and Ice Please.

02

Performance marketing

The full stack we already run for Golden Aces, Rajdarbar, Pacompro and Isthara. Lead generation ads for franchise, corporate gifting and B2B pipelines, plus reach, engagement and conversion campaigns for footfall, store orders and gifting sales, not vanity metrics.

03

In house food shoots & reels

Our own studio and crew. Sizzle reels, hero plating and outlet shoots produced in house, no third party hand offs.

04

Campaign ideation & strategy

Strategy first: GTM, positioning and a festive calendar mapped to your real sales cycle and business objectives.

05

Launches, seasonal & outlet bursts

From a Diwali gifting push to a new outlet opening, concept to live in days, with creative built per occasion.

06

Consumer first food storytelling

We speak the audience's language, region by region, local and meme fluent, never one generic tone. Scroll Isthara's feed and you'll hear it.

Why us, specifically

You listed three preferences. We tick all three.

Based in Delhi NCR

Headquartered in Delhi & Agra. On ground for shoots, outlet visits and quick turnarounds, not managing your brand from another time zone.

Already in fresh food & QSR

Running Isthara (multi brand food courts, 60+ outlets, 200+ brand partners), Koreatown (Korean BBQ QSR) and Ice Please (premium cocktail ice) right now. We live in this category.

Strategic + execution partner

We own the strategy, the camera and the ad account. One team, end to end, page management is just the beginning of what we do.

Case study 01 · multi brand diner

Isthara, turning a food court into a feed people follow.

Social managementReelsFestive moment marketingPerformance

A B2B leaning operator of multi brand food courts, now 60+ outlets across India, needed a consumer facing brand voice. So we built a region first, craving led content engine: not one generic tone, but content tuned to each location's language and taste, from South Indian markets like Karnataka to North Indian cities like Meerut. Meme reels, festive moments (Ganesh Chaturthi, Rose Day) and cross brand showcases turn "200+ brands under one roof" into something you actually want to eat.

The account, on the phone

~47K community · 385 posts
A grid we art direct
Every format, on brand
+25.8% in 90 days
1.08M views · 1.36M reached

Reels that travelled , tap to watch on Instagram

Experience Choice ↗
Breakfast · Medu Vada ↗

Scroll stopping posts

View the full feed

The content buckets we ran , what each one fixed

01

Region first meme reels

Relatable food humour in each outlet's local language.

ProblemA B2B food court operator sounded corporate, no reason for diners to follow.
SolvedLocally voiced meme reels gave each city a reason to share, pushing reach far beyond followers.

02

Cross brand "Experience Choice"

Thali & variety reels showing many cuisines in one place.

Problem"200+ brands under one roof" was a claim, not a craving.
SolvedShowcase reels made the sheer choice feel mouth watering instead of abstract.

03

Festive moment marketing

Timely creatives mapped to the cultural calendar.

ProblemThe brand kept missing the moments people were already talking about.
SolvedGanesh Chaturthi modak (136 likes), Rose Day gulab jamun bouquet & more landed on trend.

04

Daypart pushes

Breakfast, lunch & late evening cravings, on cue.

ProblemFootfall sagged outside the lunch rush.
SolvedDaypart content (like the medu vada breakfast reel) nudged off peak visits.

05

Food cinematography

Styled hero shots & appetising close ups.

ProblemPhone shot food looked flat and unappetising.
SolvedArt directed framing made everyday dishes look genuinely crave worthy.

06

Dish & menu spotlights

Rotating focus on signature and new dishes.

ProblemStrong menu items got lost in a busy multi brand line up.
SolvedA spotlight cadence gave individual dishes their own airtime.

07

Offer & combo creatives

Clear, scroll stopping deal posts.

ProblemDiscounts buried in captions never converted.
SolvedSharp offer creatives tied directly into performance ads.

08

Community & UGC

Diner photos, reviews & reposts.

ProblemLittle social proof, so newcomers hesitated.
SolvedResharing real diner content built trust and a sense of belonging.

09

Trend & audio led reels

Riding trending formats and sounds.

ProblemOrganic reach had plateaued.
SolvedTrend jacking sent reels travelling, 95.5% of reach came from non followers.

10

Performance backed amplification

Paid reach + retargeting on the best organic.

ProblemPosts only ever reached the people already following.
SolvedSmart amplification (94% of reach from ads) grew the page +25.8% in 90 days.

The brief was never just social. People remember the restaurants they eat at, almost never the food court they ate in. Our job was to build a brand diners would recognise, trust and seek out by name, the way they do a global chain, in every city, at every outlet.

So we started at zero, and ended up running the whole thing.

We built the brand from the ground up

Before a single reel there was a brand to make. Strategy, a name-led identity, and a voice consistent enough that an outlet in Hyderabad and one in Delhi feel like the same Isthara.

Brand strategy & positioning Logo & visual identity Brand guidelines Communication framework Brand voice & messaging

Marketing that doubles as business development

The unusual part: the work doesn't stop at diners. The same engine feeds Isthara's expansion, dropping qualified leads straight into the pipeline.

Expand

Franchise leads

Inquiries, not impressions

Campaigns built to generate franchise inquiries and support the national rollout, city by city.

Fill

Brand & kitchen partners

Stocking the food halls

Drives aimed at restaurant brands, cloud kitchens and F&B businesses looking to grow through Isthara's network.

Secure

Real-estate & sites

Lining up the next outlets

Lead generation aimed at developers, landlords and property owners to fuel the next wave of locations.

A full brand ecosystem, not just a feed

Every touchpoint a diner, partner or investor meets is something we've designed, online and on the ground.

Digital

The online experience

Website strategy, UI/UX, user-journey and conversion design, the whole digital brand experience.

Corporate

The growth deck

Company profiles, pitch and investor decks, franchise presentations and business-development collateral.

Offline

The real world

Billboards, hoardings, signage, flyers, in-store and interior branding systems, launch collateral.

From launches to long-term growth

Every new outlet and every new food partner gets its own launch plan, so each opening turns into visibility and footfall, not just a ribbon cut. Years in, we're still the team Isthara grows with.

From just another food court to a name people recognise: 60+ outlets across India, 200+ food brands under one roof, a footprint from Delhi, Hyderabad and Chennai into emerging markets, and an end-to-end brand ecosystem we built and still run, strategy to signage.

Case study 02 · Korean BBQ · QSR

Koreatown @ Foyer, food you can almost hear sizzle.

For RajDarbar RealtyIn house videographyFood cinematicsReels

High production, appetite driven video from our in house crew: comic style chef sizzle reels, levitating hot pot hero shots, and BBQ and beer lifestyle. Built to make a destination QSR feel premium and genuinely crave worthy, exactly the in house shoot capability your brief asks for.

The content buckets we ran , what each one fixed

01

Sizzle & ASMR reels

Sound led cooking moments that trigger appetite.

ProblemKorean food was unfamiliar, people couldn't "taste" it through a screen.
SolvedSizzle and ASMR reels made the craving instant and obvious.

02

Hot pot hero cinematics

High production signature dish films.

ProblemSignature dishes looked ordinary in standard photos.
SolvedLevitating hot pot hero shots turned a dish into an event.

03

BBQ + beer lifestyle

Group dining & night out energy.

ProblemThe venue read as "just a restaurant," not a night out.
SolvedBBQ and chilled beer films positioned it as a destination hangout.

04

Character led storytelling

A comic style chef as a recurring face.

ProblemGeneric food content blended into the feed.
SolvedA comic chef character gave the brand a scroll stopping identity.

05

Cuisine education

Light "how to eat it" explainers.

ProblemFirst timers felt intimidated by the menu.
SolvedSimple explainer content lowered the barrier to trying Korean BBQ.

06

Menu launches & specials

Anticipation building drops.

ProblemNew dishes launched to silence.
SolvedRepeatable launch creative built hype before each special went live.

07

Ambience & experience

Interiors, plating & the in store vibe.

ProblemA key differentiator, the space, was invisible online.
SolvedAmbience led reels showed the experience, not just the food.

08

Offers & footfall drivers

Happy hour & weekday creatives.

ProblemWeekday footfall was soft.
SolvedTargeted offer content filled the quieter slots.

09

Trend led reels

Local relevant trending audio & formats.

ProblemDiscovery was limited to people who already knew the place.
SolvedTrend led reels reached fresh local audiences.

10

Premium brand building

Consistent, high production look & feel.

ProblemIt risked feeling like just another QSR.
SolvedA polished, cinematic style lifted the brand's perceived value.

Case study 03 · premium craft ice · D2C

Ice Please, making a commodity feel crave worthy.

Carousel designCelebrity reelsCocktail contentConcept creatives

Ice Please! is a premium craft ice brand, crystal clear, slow melting, customisable cubes made with RO+UV filtered water and 0% hand touch, built for cocktails, bars, weddings and gifting and sold D2C at iceplease.in. Ice is the definition of an invisible commodity, so we turned it into a desirable lifestyle brand: we translated "pure, slow melting, customisable" into content people actually want, recipe carousels, a celebrity chill campaign, bartender approved credibility, heat led summer hooks, and scroll stopping concept creatives (yes, a diamond frozen mid cube). Today the feed pulls 584K+ views a month, with posts that travel into the hundreds of thousands.

The account, on the phone

9,939 followers · 584K views / 30 days

The content buckets we ran , what each one fixed

01

Cocktail recipe carousels

Swipe through builds like Reverie, Escape & After Glow.

ProblemPeople didn't know what to actually do with premium ice.
SolvedStep by step recipe carousels showed exactly how to build a drink around it.

02

Celebrity & influencer reels

Star led "we brought the chill" moments.

ProblemA new ice brand had no aspirational pull.
SolvedCelebrity led reels delivered instant reach and a premium halo.

03

Bartender approved credibility

Expert hands, clear ice technique.

Problem"Why pay for fancy ice?" scepticism.
SolvedBartender content built authority and justified the premium.

04

Concept "frozen in ice" creatives

Diamonds, roses & flowers set in cubes.

ProblemIce reads as a boring, invisible commodity.
SolvedStriking conceptual shots made it instantly share- and gift worthy.

05

Weather & seasonal hooks

"42° everywhere, chill right here."

ProblemNo urgent reason to buy right now.
SolvedHeat led creatives tied the product to summer and poolside occasions.

06

Product hero & texture

Macro shots of crystal clear, slow melt cubes.

ProblemClarity and quality are hard to convey in words.
SolvedDetailed hero shots let the craftsmanship speak for itself.

07

Occasion & lifestyle

Rooftops, pool parties, date nights.

ProblemUnclear when and where to use it.
SolvedLifestyle content placed it inside real celebration moments.

08

Gifting & statement angle

"Say it with Ice Please."

ProblemA narrow use case capped demand.
SolvedPositioned infused ice as a gift and a statement for occasions.

09

Trend & meme reels

Gen Z language, "rizz", and more.

ProblemHard for a premium brand to feel current.
SolvedPlayful trend jacking kept it culturally relevant and fun.

10

Education / how to use

Why clear ice melts slower & tastes cleaner.

ProblemConsumers didn't understand the benefit.
SolvedSimple explainers showed how craft ice elevates the whole drink.

Before we pitch · your handles, audited

We pulled apart all eight Haldiram's handles before writing a word.

Eight public handles, three regions, one nearly-$10-billion brand. We went through every account the way a new follower would, what gets posted, how often, what travels and what stalls. The content is genuinely good. But for a brand this size, it's quiet, scattered and poster-led, and there's no single front door.

410+
Products: snacks, sweets, RTE & drinks
endless content, under-shot
80+
Countries it exports to
a global story, told quietly
8
Public Instagram handles
with no single front door

Brand figures from public filings & press, 2024 to 2026. We can't see your private Instagram insights, those need your login, so the numbers above are brand facts and category benchmarks, not your dashboard. Plug in your analytics and we'll sharpen every figure.

The eight handles, and what we'd change on each

INDIA · FLAGSHIP

@haldirams.official

The master brand, festive films, big campaigns, the corporate face, and the storytelling genuinely works.

KeepStrong equity and festive storytelling, exactly as is.
Bio"One-stop destination for your food cravings" is too generic, and it links only Swiggy when you're on Zomato too. Swap in one link that opens every platform, Swiggy, Zomato and the D2C store.
HighlightsMake the highlight covers a map of your footprint, one circle per country Haldiram's is in.
CoversEvery new-outlet reel should put the exact location and a clear one-line message on the cover, so the grid itself tells people where the store just opened.
UGCCollabs read as paid ads. Brief creators to play, e.g. Ayush Sapra takes the summer menu as a challenge, "I'll try every dish and find the best." Product woven in, not announced.

INDIA · PRODUCT

@haldirams_snacks

The snacking handle, bhujia, namkeen, RTE, the everyday craving.

KeepAn infinite, reel-native product library.
LaneIt's posting the same snack content as @haldiraminternational. Keep this handle the pure product-and-craving engine, and hand the geography story across to International.

GLOBAL · EXPORT

@haldiraminternational

The diaspora and 80-country export story.

KeepA real reason to exist as the brand goes global.
PlaceStop reposting the same snacks as @haldirams_snacks. This handle's only job is place, which countries Haldiram's is in and exactly where to find the stores and products. Every post should answer "where can I get it."

INDIA · DINE-IN

@haldirams_restaurants

The QSR and restaurant handle, menu, dining, footfall. Overall it looks good.

KeepSolid feed and a clear footfall job.
CopySome static posts just label the dish over a photo. A brand this size can write witty, characterful copy on every one.
ChefsFar too few chef posts. The people behind the counter are magnetic, the cake-section chef at the Gurgaon outlet alone can hold a camera instantly. Put the chefs on screen.

BEVERAGE · GEN-Z

@haldiramsthanda

Thanda Funda, flavoured milk, thandai, summer drinks. The youngest, most Gen-Z-ready sub-brand.

KeepThe brand has shown it can trend (the Labubu summer push), the potential is real.
ContentThis handle is mostly AI-generated stills with no reels, it'll barely catch anyone. It needs real, human-shot content and reels, or a proper brand film to give the drinks a personality.

UK · MARKET

@haldiramsuk

The UK retail and diaspora handle. Looks good.

KeepGenuinely solid for the market.
ChefsSame gap as the India dine-in handle, bring in far more chef-led content.

UK · DINE-IN

@haldiramsuk.restaurants

UK restaurant footfall and the dine-in experience.

KeepHyper-local intent, the people most likely to walk in.
CadenceThe thinnest of the eight, it needs a steady local rhythm of store, event and chef content so it doesn't drift toward dormancy.

UAE · MARKET

@haldiramsuae

The Gulf handle, retail, dining and high-value diaspora.

KeepOne of the richest diaspora markets on earth.
FixUnder-leveraged, Ramadan and Eid gifting alone should make this a powerhouse handle.

Head-to-head: Haldiram's vs Bikanervala

We ran both brands through Rival IQ for the same two weeks. The split is the whole story, one platform you're quietly winning, the other you're clearly not.

Instagram
Jun 12–26, 2026 · @haldirams.official vs @bikanervala.in
Engagement per post
413
Haldiram's
Winner · 4.6×
vs
89.8
Bikanervala
165k followers
1.5 posts / wk177k followers
5 posts / wk

The content works; the cadence doesn't. You out-engage Bikanervala 4.6× per post on barely a third of the posting. Post more often and that lead compounds fast.

Facebook
Jun 12–26, 2026 · "Haldiram's Nagpur" vs Bikanervala
Engagement per post
14.4
Haldiram's
vs
103
Bikanervala
Winner · 7×
122k audience
5 posts / wk268k audience
5 posts / wk

Same effort, a fraction of the return. Identical posting, yet 7× less engagement, half the audience and 6× fewer shares, all under a regional page name. This is the rebuild priority.

Source: Rival IQ Head-to-Head reports, Instagram & Facebook, Jun 12–26 2026 — Haldiram's vs Bikanervala.

Why Facebook lags: architecture. The page above is named "Haldiram's Nagpur", a regional, legacy name reading as a single page, not a master brand. So Instagram is over-split across eight handles while Facebook is under-built and regionally labelled. Same root cause on both: no clear brand architecture.
The one thing Bikanervala does better. Their thumbnail game is sorted, every new-location reel cover states exactly where the store is opening, with a clear written message on it. It's the simplest win on this whole list, and the first thing we'd ship.

Across all eight, the same four habits repeat. The feed is poster-led when the algorithm rewards reels. It's campaign-burst, not always-on. It's one tone stretched over regions that each deserve their own. And there's no single front door, a new fan genuinely can't tell which handle is "Haldiram's".

Even outside analysts call the brand's marketing too quiet for its size, and flag how little of South India it speaks to. And too much of it is faceless, AI-generated stills and collabs that announce themselves as ads, when your own chefs on camera and a lighter, challenge-style creator touch would travel far further. For a name the world just valued near $10 billion, the feed is running three weight classes below the business.

Five gaps we'd close in the first 30 days

Gap 01

Reels-first, not poster-first

Food is the most reel-native category on the platform. We'd flip the ratio toward roughly 70% reels, crunch, pour, sizzle, hunger, and treat static as support, not the main act.

Gap 02

One architecture, one front door

Name @haldirams.official the master handle and the rest as clearly-labelled spokes: consistent bios, cross-links and one bio link that opens every ordering platform (Swiggy and Zomato, not just one), so followers never get lost between eight accounts.

Gap 03

Region & language, not one tone

Hinglish and regional cuts for India, genuinely local content for the UK & UAE, and a real South-India push, the exact gap analysts keep pointing at.

Gap 04

Put people on screen, starting with your chefs

Your own chefs are magnetic and barely feature, the kind who can hold a camera instantly. Pair them with creators briefed to play, not advertise, challenge-style collabs where the product is woven in, never announced. Daily Stories, replies and UGC reposts turn it into always-on volume.

Gap 05

Content wired to commerce

Every craveable reel should land somewhere: haldiramsonline.com, quick commerce (Blinkit, Zepto, Instamart) "in 10 minutes", gifting hampers or store footfall. The Sept-2025 GST price cut is a ready-made "now more pocket-friendly" hook.

So here's the fix

The feed a $10 billion snack brand should actually have.

Same heritage, same 410-product catalogue, rebuilt for how people watch food in 2026. Here's the content engine we'd run across all eight handles, and exactly where it turns into revenue.

The reel formats we'd run every week

01

ASMR crunch & pour

Bhujia snap, thandai pour, gulab-jamun syrup, tight close-up, sound-on.

DrivesSaves and non-follower reach. · @haldirams_snacks, @haldiramsthanda

02

"Bhook lagi?" hunger skits

Relatable Hinglish hunger moments, the everyday "I need a snack now".

DrivesShares and brand love with Gen-Z. · @haldirams_snacks

03

Minute Khana, ready in 2

Hostel, office and travel RTE demos, hot meal in ninety seconds.

DrivesD2C & quick-commerce conversion. · @haldirams.official

04

Festival moment-marketing

Diwali, Raksha Bandhan, Holi, Ramadan & Eid, region by region, on trending audio.

DrivesPeak-window reach and sales. · all handles

05

Gifting & hamper films

Premium festive and corporate gift boxes, styled and unboxed.

DrivesGifting-season ROAS and B2B leads. · @haldirams.official, @haldiramsuae

06

Thanda Funda, pop-culture

Keep the Labubu-style energy year-round, with seasonal flavour drops.

DrivesYoung-audience recruitment. · @haldiramsthanda

07

"Since 1937" heritage reels

The Bikaner origin, the craft, the scale behind 410+ products.

DrivesTrust and premium perception. · @haldiraminternational, @haldirams.official

08

Creator challenges & chef's table

Creators briefed to play (the summer-menu challenge, not a paid-looking post), plus your own chefs on camera doing what they do best.

DrivesAuthentic social proof and human volume. · @haldirams_restaurants, @haldiramsuk

Where the feed turns into revenue

D2C

Q-COMM

Quick commerce

Blinkit, Zepto & Instamart "order in 10 minutes" content plus geo-targeted ads, impulse snacking is built for it.

GLOBAL

International expansion

B2B

Gifting & pipeline

UnlocksA qualified sales pipeline, not just impressions.

That's the homework. The rest of this page is the plan, the campaigns, the in-house shoots, the four-playbook performance engine and a six-month calendar mapped handle by handle.

We didn't pull apart your feed to criticise it. We did it because we'd rather walk in already knowing exactly where the growth is hiding.

On method: Instagram's public pages block automated tools, so we read the eight handles the way any new follower would and benchmarked against food and FMCG norms. Brand figures (valuation, revenue, market share, product count) are from public filings and press, 2024 to 2026. Give us access to your professional insights and every number here gets sharper.

The engagement

How we'd run Haldiram's, from day one.

One team handling the whole loop, from the strategy deck to the camera to the ad dashboard. Here's the shape of it.

Always on social studio

Feed, reels, stories and community, one brand, but a voice tuned to each region and language across every Haldiram's handle.

Performance engine

Lead generation, reach, engagement and conversion ads. D2C for haldiramsonline.com, footfall for outlets, gifting season ROAS, plus a CRM-feeding lead layer for corporate gifting, franchise and B2B. The full stack we already run for Rajdarbar, Pacompro, Golden Aces and Isthara.

In house food content

Product hero shots, Minute Khana RTE demos, namkeen close ups and sweet box gifting films, shot by our crew, not outsourced.

Festive command centre

Diwali, Raksha Bandhan, Holi, Navratri, Ganesh Chaturthi, calendars and creative mapped to your peak sales windows.

Launch & outlet playbooks

New SKU launches, store openings and regional bursts, repeatable creative kits that go live in days.

A festive calendar, built around your catalogue

SpringHoliGujiya, thandai & colourful snack hampers.
MonsoonRaksha BandhanSweet box gifting, kaju katli & soan papdi.
Late summerGanesh ChaturthiModak moment, the post that did 136 likes for Isthara.
AutumnNavratriVrat friendly & fasting snack ranges.
Festive peakDiwaliGift hampers, tin sweets & the big ROAS push.
WinterWinter SpecialGajak, gur & rewri, straight from your menu.
The performance engine

Ads that move the number, not just the feed.

We don't pitch creative without performance. Every campaign we run carries an ad layer underneath, lead generation when the brand needs a pipeline, reach when it needs to be seen, engagement when it needs to grow, and conversion when it needs to sell. Four playbooks, all already running on our books.

The four playbooks, in plain English

Lead generation ads

Pipeline ads for B2B sales, franchise inquiries, real estate, food court partners, tournament signups, anything that needs to land in a CRM. We run Meta and Google lead forms, instant forms, lookalikes off your customer base. Tracked on CPL, qualified leads, MQL to SQL.

Reach & awareness ads

Top-of-funnel for brand launches, new SKUs, festive pushes, city expansions and brand films. Built for scale, frequency and recall. Tracked on CPM, video views, view-through rates and brand search lift.

Engagement & community ads

Follower growth, post amplification, profile-visit campaigns. Turns scrollers into followers and followers into super-fans. Tracked on cost per follower, engagement rate, save and share velocity.

Conversion & sales ads

Bottom-of-funnel for D2C purchases, sign-ups, app installs, online sales. Built around the cart, the catalogue and retargeting pools. Tracked on ROAS, CPA, AOV and repeat purchase rate.

Brands we already run performance for

Golden Aces

50K+ main · 5K+ each city page

Lead generation ads for tournament signups across markets. Reach + engagement campaigns that grew the main page past 50K and built a 5K+ following on every city page we launched.

Rajdarbar Reality

Real estate sales pipeline

Lead generation ads for property inquiries. Geo-targeted to high-intent buyers, qualified at the form stage, fed straight to the sales team. A pipeline, not just impressions.

Pacompro

Franchise leads + product sales

Two funnels running in parallel. Lead generation ads to build the franchise partner network, plus conversion ads driving online product sales. The full performance loop in one account.

Isthara Food Courts

Food court partner inquiries

Lead generation ads for food court partner sign-ups across cities, with the social engine you've already seen above. The leads behind the case study.

+ many more startups across food, retail, real estate and services.

For Haldiram's, all four playbooks run at once: Lead ads for corporate gifting and franchise inquiries. Reach for the brand film. Engagement for handle growth across all five. Conversion for haldiramsonline.com, and the gifting season ROAS push.
The big idea

Three campaigns. One brand.

A brand the size of Haldiram's doesn't need one campaign. It needs an architecture, a brand platform that lives forever, and two product platforms that own their categories. Each one ladders up to a Haldiram's truth no competitor can claim. Each one runs on a specific handle. None of them borrow the shape of "kuch meetha ho jaye" or "khushiyan ho toh Haldiram's." This is sentences only Haldiram's can say.

Campaign 01 · Brand platform

Bas ek aur.

The line every Indian has already said.

Nobody eats one bhujia. One kaju katli. One mathri. The hand always goes back. Ninety years of one more. Festive lockups: Bas ek aur box (Diwali) and Bas ek aur bite (Rakhi).

Anchors: @haldirams.official · echoes across all five

Campaign 02 · Snacks

Hindustan ka Crunch.

The sound only India makes when she eats.

From bhujia to wafers to mathri to chana jor garam, every Haldiram's snack lives on one thing, the kadak. We own the sound. Sound-led, reels-first, ASMR by design.

Anchors: @haldirams_snacks · echoes on @haldiraminternational

Campaign 03 · Packaged meals

Bhook lagi? Haldiram's hai na.

India doesn't have meal times. India has hunger times.

9pm guests. 2am hostel hunger. Train delays. Post-flight cravings. There's one answer to every "what now?" moment, and it's the Minute Khana shelf, the freezer, the packet.

Anchors: @haldirams.official (RTE) · @haldirams_restaurants (dine-in)

Campaign 01 · The brand film

Bas ek aur.

A 90-second brand film. One sentence every Indian has already said about Haldiram's. We're not coining it. We're naming what's been there since 1937, and giving it the film it deserves.

The voiceover script

Nobody has ever eaten one piece of bhujia. One kaju katli. One mathri. The hand always goes back. Always.

From the office colleague who "just samples" your dabba, to the diet uncle who says "kal se pakka, promise", to the kid sneaking to dadi's tin at midnight, only one brand makes every Indian say the same three words out loud.

Bas ek aur. Bas ek aur. Bas ek aur.

Ninety years of one more.

The film, scene by scene

One film. Ten Indian moments. The voiceover above runs continuous, the camera weaves through these ten vignettes, each one only a few seconds long, each one a different Haldiram's reach. Black frame open, the line, the ninety-year mark, end.

Open

Black frame. Sound only.

Faint crackle of bhujia. The first three words of the voiceover. Then the first cut.

Scene 01

The Office Dabba

Colleague hovers over your tiffin. "Bas ek aur try kar lo, daal mein hi toh hai." Cut to: tiffin empty.

Scene 02

Dadi's Almirah at Midnight

Kid sneaks to the bhujia tin. Dadi's already there. They lock eyes. Both reach in. Bas ek aur.

Scene 03

Diet Uncle, Kal Se Pakka

Treadmill on, mathri in hand, wife filming. "Bas ek aur, kal se pakka, promise." Cut to next morning. Same scene.

Scene 04

The Bride and the Chaat Counter

Her own wedding. The chaat counter knows her order. She keeps going back, lehenga and all. Groom watches, smiling, defeated.

Scene 05

The Care Package

Mom in Delhi packs a Haldiram's box for the son in NYC. Video call. He's already opened it. "Bas ek aur khaa le, bachega kya?"

Scene 06

The Rajdhani Express

Family opens one packet. Every co-passenger reaches in. Strangers become bas ek aur friends. By Bhopal, the packet is empty.

Scene 07

The Zoom Mute

Camera on, mic off, hand sneaking to the bhujia packet during a board review. We've all been there. The CFO is on mute too.

Scene 08

The IPL Wicket

Every wicket = bas ek aur namkeen. Every sixer = bas ek aur. Match ends in a super over. Packet ends three overs in.

Scene 09

The Diwali Box

Family dinner. Box opened. Every relative says "bas ek aur." Box empty in twelve minutes. The setup for the cutdown below.

Scene 10

The Family Dog

Even the family dog sits patient and unblinking by the kaju katli plate. Tail wagging. A single piece left. The dog wins.

End

"Bas ek aur."

Black frame. The line, white type, centre. Below it: "Haldiram's. Ninety years of one more." Logo. Cut.

Two 30-second cutdowns, from the same film

Built from the same footage, the same voiceover treatment, the same end frame. Two lockups that arrive on schedule, every year, the same week the brand wants to dominate.

Diwali cutdown

Bas ek aur box.

Pulls scenes 05 and 09 (the care package, the Diwali dinner). Closes on "Bas ek aur box," then the Haldiram's gift box hero, then the line. The gifting season's anthem, lifted from the brand film, ready in 30 seconds.

Rakhi cutdown

Bas ek aur bite.

Pulls scene 04 (the bride at the chaat counter) and a fresh shoot of a brother reaching across the Rakhi thaali. Closes on "Bas ek aur bite," then the kaju katli hero, then the line. The Rakhi spot the category doesn't have yet.

Anchors on: @haldirams.official Echoes on: @haldirams_snacks @haldirams_restaurants @haldirams_nagpur @haldiraminternational
Campaign 02 · The snacks platform

Hindustan ka Crunch.

A sound-led platform for the snacks lane. Aloo bhujia, wafers, mathri, chana, every product Haldiram's makes that kadaks in your teeth.

There's a sound only India makes when she eats. Kadak. That tiny moment between teeth meeting bhujia and bhujia surrendering. The snap of mathri. The crackle of chana jor garam.

From Bikaner to Bengaluru, Boston to Bahrain, that sound is India.

Haldiram's built it. Haldiram's owns it.

Hindustan ka Crunch.

The content territory, sound-on by design

Idea 01

The Sound Test

Pin-drop silent studio. One bite. The mic captures the kadak. No music, no VO, just the snap. Hero asset for the launch reel.

Idea 02

Crunch Cities

Same bhujia, eaten in Mumbai, Chennai, Lucknow, Imphal, Kochi. Same crunch, different cities, same sound. A weekly series.

Idea 03

The 30-Day Crunch

Day 1 vs Day 30 from the same packet. Same crunch. Slow-mo. Audio comparison. The shelf-life flex, packaged as a film.

Idea 04

Guess the Snack

Audio-only reels. Bhujia? Mathri? Wafer? Comment to guess. Engagement bait, but the kadak earns the comment.

Idea 05

The Crunch Off

Haldiram's vs the unbranded imitator. Sound-on test. You'll hear which is which before you see it. We don't say it. The audio does.

Idea 06

Crunch in Every Language

Bhujia eaters across India calling out the sound in their own language. Kadak, mokra, parukh, karkar, kurkure. One crunch, many words.

Idea 07

Crunchy History

1937 Bikaner kitchen. Same recipe, same crunch, ninety years later. Heritage delivered in a snap, not a long film.

Idea 08

The ASMR Edit

Full mic, no music, slow-mo wafer snap, mathri break, chana crackle. A monthly cinematic for the sound-led feed.

The product range it carries

Aloo Bhujia Bhujia Sev Moong Dal Namkeen Khatta Meetha Aloo Lachha Wafers Mathri Khakhra Chana Jor Garam Chivda
Anchors on: @haldirams_snacks Echoes on: @haldiraminternational @haldirams_nagpur
Campaign 03 · The packaged meals platform

Bhook lagi? Haldiram's hai na.

A platform for Minute Khana, frozen and the ready-to-eat shelf. Shahi paneer, dal makhani, chole, rajma, biryani, every reach-for-it-now product the brand makes.

India doesn't have meal times. India has hunger times.

Hunger at 9pm when guests show up. Hunger at 2am when the hostel kitchen's shut. Hunger on the train when the pantry car never comes. Hunger after a 12-hour flight. Hunger after a fight with your mother. Hunger when you don't even know why you're hungry.

There is exactly one answer to all of it. A pantry, a freezer, a packet, ninety seconds.

Bhook lagi? Haldiram's hai na.

The hunger moments, eight situations to start

Moment 01

9pm, Surprise Guests

Relatives at the door without notice. Fridge empty. Cabinet has Minute Khana shahi paneer. Six minutes later, full thaali. Reputation intact.

Moment 02

The Hostel 2am

Hostel mess shut. Canteen closed. Roommate slides over a Minute Khana chole bhature. The microwave knows what to do.

Moment 03

The Train Pantry

Rajdhani running four hours late. Pantry car never showed. You open your bag. Dal makhani, rajma, biryani. Strangers watch jealously.

Moment 04

The WFH Lunch Break

Call ends at 1:00. Next call at 1:15. Minute Khana, microwave, plate. You log back on with food, not excuses.

Moment 05

The Bachelor Rajma

Tried to make it from scratch. Burnt the pressure cooker. Roommate quietly tears open a Minute Khana. Reality, acknowledged.

Moment 06

The Post-Fight Peace

Fought with mom. Sulking in the room. Door creaks open. Plate slides in. Minute Khana chole. The closest thing to an apology in this house.

Moment 07

The 3am Landing

12-hour flight. Landed Delhi 3am. Nothing's open, even the swiggy delivery is shut. Pantry has Haldiram's. Welcome home, actually.

Moment 08

The Cricket Match

India batting at 7pm. No time to cook. Minute Khana plus remote = match-day setup. Toss done in less time than the dal.

The product range it carries

Shahi Paneer Dal Makhani Chole Rajma Pav Bhaji Biryani Khichdi Pulao Frozen Samosa Frozen Kachori
Anchors on: @haldirams.official Dine-in interpretation on: @haldirams_restaurants
The 6-month roadmap

A 6-month plan, by handle.

Each handle leads with one of the three campaigns, alongside the festive moments. So a follower on @haldirams_snacks gets the crunch story, a follower on @haldirams_restaurants gets the dine-in story, and the main handle anchors the brand film and the Minute Khana hunger moments. Each month carries a clear theme and a deliverable.

Always on, every handle, every month: daily stories, community replies, reels-first cadence and an ad engine running underneath.
Track 01 · Main flagship

@haldirams.official

Leads with Bas ek aur (brand film) + Bhook lagi? Haldiram's hai na (Minute Khana)
JulM01

Manifesto film launch. "Bas ek aur" hero film + 6s cutdowns. Monsoon Minute Khana pakora moments.

AugM02

"Bas ek aur bite" launches for Rakhi. I-Day cut: "75 saal of one more." UGC engine live.

SepM03

"Bas ek aur modak" for Ganesh. Daily Minute Khana RTE demos. Diwali audience warmup.

OctM04

Vrat-friendly Minute Khana for Navratri. "Bhook lagi? Vrat hai? Hai na." Diwali film teasers.

NovM05

"Bas ek aur box" Diwali campaign at full scale. Corporate gifting push, D2C ROAS peak.

DecM06

"Bas ek aur saal" year-in-cravings recap film. Winter Bhook lagi? menu. New Year anthem.

Track 02 · Snacks & sweets

@haldirams_snacks

Leads with Hindustan ka Crunch (sound-led snacks platform)
JulM01

"Hindustan ka Crunch" launch with a 6-product sound reel. Bhujia ASMR. Mathri snap.

AugM02

Tricolour Crunch for I-Day (three regional namkeens). Rakhi sweets storytelling.

SepM03

Crunch Cities series begins. Onam banana chips push. Ganesh laddoo close-ups.

OctM04

Vrat Crunch — sabudana, makhana. Wafer × Diwali movie-night content. Heavy reels output.

NovM05

Sweets gifting hero — kaju katli, soan papdi, namkeen hamper films. Diwali conversion ads.

DecM06

Winter crunch — gajak, gur rewri. "Crunch of the year" recap. New Year handover.

Track 03 · Restaurants

@haldirams_restaurants

Leads with Bhook lagi? Haldiram's hai na (dine-in) + Bas ek aur (experience cuts)
JulM01

Monsoon menu + "Bhook lagi? Restaurant hai na" hyperlocal reels per outlet.

AugM02

Rakhi family dine-in films. I-Day thaali specials. Outlet-level UGC.

SepM03

Festival thaalis (Ganesh, Onam). "Bas ek aur plate" ambience cinematics begin.

OctM04

Navratri thaalis. Pre-Diwali office party bookings. Late-night "Bhook lagi?" reels.

NovM05

Corporate Diwali parties bookings push. Post-Diwali wedding catering reels.

DecM06

Winter menu launches. New Year dinner promo reels. Outlet-by-outlet scorecard.

Track 04 · Regional flagship

@haldirams_nagpur

Leads with All three campaigns, in Marathi + heritage voice (the 1937 angle)
JulM01

Local "Bas ek aur" with Marathi voiceover. Cadence reset, fewer sharper posts.

AugM02

"Nagpur ki kaju katli, 1937 se" Rakhi heritage angle. I-Day with regional pride.

SepM03

Ganesh Chaturthi peak — Maharashtra's festival, the brand's moment. Factory modak backstory.

OctM04

Navratri Garba content. Heritage Diwali build, "the original Haldiram's" push.

NovM05

"Same swaad since 1937." Heritage gifting reels. Diwali conversion.

DecM06

Winter ranges + 90-year heritage recap film. Q1 plan handover.

Track 05 · International / US diaspora

@haldiraminternational

Leads with Bas ek aur (NRI care-package cut) + India's Crunch (Hindustan ka Crunch, exported)
JulM01

NRI "Bas ek aur" care package film. "India's Crunch" landing. US summer BBQ + bhujia content.

AugM02

Long-distance Rakhi gifting films, "Bas ek aur bite, from home." I-Day for diaspora.

SepM03

Early Diwali shipping push. "Crunch of home" series. Cricket-season snacking ties.

OctM04

NRI Diwali gifting films. US corporate gifting angle. International courier ads.

NovM05

US Diwali peak. "Bas ek aur box, shipped with love." Thanksgiving × Haldiram's content.

DecM06

Holiday gifting overlap. New Year NRI cravings recap. 2027 plan.
